title: My EB1C priority date became current a few years ago, but I did not file my Form I-485 at that time. I later entered the U.S. in L1A status and planned to apply for adjustment of status with my family. Is there any issue with applying for a green card after waiting a few years after my priority date became current? - Murthy Law Firm | U.S Immigration Law
content:
## My EB1C priority date became current a few years ago, but I did not file my Form I-485 at that time. I later entered the U.S. in L1A status and planned to apply for adjustment of status with my family. Is there any issue with applying for a green card after waiting a few years after my priority date became current?
As long as the qualifying job offer underlying the approved Form I-140 immigrant petition remains valid, there is typically no specific deadline by which a person must file the Form I-485 application for adjustment of status after the priority date becomes current. (19.May.2026)
